title: Fixes bug whereby read_only bool is called as a function
body:
This is a simple bug fix to remedy the following issue:

```python
  File ".../site-packages/prompt_toolkit/filters/app.py", line 374, in emacs_insert_mode
    or app.current_buffer.read_only()
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Exception 'bool' object is not callable
```

It would appear to be a simple typo, calling the boolean `read_only` property as if it were a function.

step_plan.summary: Replace direct calls to buffer.read_only() in prompt_toolkit with is_true(buffer.read_only) so both boolean values and Filter instances are evaluated safely; add necessary imports and update buffer and filter modules to avoid 'bool' object not callable errors.

steps.operation: Replace callable read_only access with is_true in is_read_only filter and add import if needed
steps.reason: is_read_only currently returns get_app().current_buffer.read_only() which calls .read_only() assuming it's always a callable Filter. If .read_only is a bool, that raises 'bool' object is not callable. Use is_true(...) to safely evaluate both Filter and bool. Also ensure is_true is imported from the package utils.
steps.side_effects: Adds a dependency on is_true import in the module. Behavior for Filter instances is preserved (is_true will evaluate the Filter correctly). Tests that previously set read_only to a boolean and hit this code path should now pass; add/adjust tests accordingly. No API change, but module import lines change and linters/type-checkers may need to be updated.

steps.operation: Replace app.current_buffer.read_only() with is_true(...) in vi_navigation_mode
steps.reason: vi_navigation_mode contains conditions like 'or app.current_buffer.read_only()' which call .read_only() directly. Using is_true(...) prevents calling a boolean and correctly handles Filter instances.
steps.side_effects: Requires is_true import in this module (if not already added by previous step). Maintains previous semantics for Filter values. Tests covering Vi navigation filters should be run; no external API change but internal filter evaluation is more robust.

steps.operation: Replace app.current_buffer.read_only() with is_true(...) in vi_insert_mode
steps.reason: vi_insert_mode has an early-return condition that calls app.current_buffer.read_only(); replace with is_true(...) to avoid TypeError when read_only is a bool.
steps.side_effects: Same as above: import of is_true required; behavior preserved for Filters. Tests for vi insert mode should be run/updated (especially tests that set buffer.read_only = True).

steps.operation: Replace app.current_buffer.read_only() with is_true(...) in vi_insert_multiple_mode
steps.reason: This function also calls app.current_buffer.read_only() in its logic; use is_true(...) to support both bool and Filter types.
steps.side_effects: Requires is_true import; no API change. Ensure tests that exercise multi-insert vi behavior with boolean read_only do not raise TypeError.

steps.operation: Replace app.current_buffer.read_only() with is_true(...) in vi_replace_mode
steps.reason: vi_replace_mode calls app.current_buffer.read_only() and can raise if read_only is a bool; is_true(...) handles both bool and Filter.
steps.side_effects: Requires is_true import. Maintain semantics for Filter objects. Run/update tests for vi replace behavior.

steps.operation: Replace app.current_buffer.read_only() with is_true(...) in vi_replace_single_mode
steps.reason: This function calls app.current_buffer.read_only(); replacing the call with is_true(...) prevents the 'bool' object not callable error and supports Filter instances properly.
steps.side_effects: Requires is_true import. No public API change. Run tests for single-character replace mode.

steps.operation: Replace app.current_buffer.read_only() with is_true(...) in emacs_insert_mode
steps.reason: emacs_insert_mode currently does 'or app.current_buffer.read_only()' which raises if read_only is a boolean. Use is_true(...) to evaluate both booleans and Filter objects robustly.
steps.side_effects: Requires is_true import. Preserves existing behavior for Filter values. Update/verify tests exercising emacs insert mode when buffer.read_only is set to a boolean.

steps.operation: Use is_true(self.read_only) in Buffer.text setter to guard against boolean read_only and add import if needed
steps.reason: The setter currently does 'if self.read_only(): raise EditReadOnlyBuffer()' which calls read_only() and fails if read_only is a bool. Replace with is_true(self.read_only) so both bool and Filter are handled. Ensure import from prompt_toolkit.filters.utils (or package-appropriate utils) is present.
steps.side_effects: Adds an import dependency on is_true in buffer.py. Preserves semantics when read_only is a Filter. Tests that set buffer.read_only = True and then attempt to set text should now raise EditReadOnlyBuffer without TypeError. Code that relied on calling an actual callable with side effects as read_only (unlikely) may be affected.

steps.operation: Use is_true(self.read_only) in Buffer.set_document to avoid calling boolean read_only
steps.reason: set_document currently checks 'if not bypass_readonly and self.read_only(): raise EditReadOnlyBuffer()'. Replace the call with is_true(self.read_only) to support boolean and Filter values.
steps.side_effects: Requires is_true import in buffer.py (if not already added). Preserves Filter semantics. Update tests that call set_document with read_only set to True to ensure correct exception is raised without TypeError.

steps.operation: Use is_true(self.read_only) in Buffer.open_in_editor to prevent TypeError when read_only is a bool
steps.reason: open_in_editor checks 'if self.read_only(): raise EditReadOnlyBuffer()' before opening the editor. Replace with is_true(self.read_only) so boolean assignments do not cause a TypeError.
steps.side_effects: Adds is_true import if missing. No API changes. Ensure tests that attempt to open editor while buffer.read_only is True now raise EditReadOnlyBuffer cleanly (no TypeError).
